title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (DFA) relatives family methods measuring long-range correlations cross-correlations nonstationary time series, classical autocorrelation ordinary regression biased trends. DFATools collects useful members family common, consistent interface: Several regression-oriented tools — scale-dependent standardized coefficients, scale-wise f2f^2effect size intersection-union test — introduced Barreto et al. (2021), whose framework package implements.
code: library(DFATools)

title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: Except dfa() (takes single numeric vector), functions expect matrix data frame whose first column response YY whose remaining columns covariates X2,…,XpX_2, \dots, X_p. share four arguments: dpo — order polynomial used detrending (1 = linear); int — TRUE, series integrated (cumulatively summed) first; np — number scales (box sizes) statistics evaluated; overlap — TRUE, sliding (overlapping) boxes used. Throughout vignette use simulated system response y depends two random-walk drivers, x2 (positive) x3 (negative):
code: n  <- 1200 x2 <- cumsum(rnorm(n)) x3 <- cumsum(rnorm(n)) y  <- 0.7 * x2 - 0.5 * x3 + cumsum(rnorm(n)) dat <- data.frame(y = y, x2 = x2, x3 = x3)

title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: DFA (Peng et al. 1994) quantifies scaling fluctuations series. series first integrated profile X(t)=∑=1t(xi−x‾)X(t) = \sum_{=1}^{t} (x_i - \bar{x}), split boxes size ss; within box polynomial trend removed variance residuals taken. Averaging boxes gives fluctuation function F(s)F(s), self-similar series follows power law F(s)∼sα, F(s) \sim s^{\alpha},  exponent α\alpha generalises Hurst exponent: α=0.5\alpha = 0.5 marks uncorrelated series, α>0.5\alpha > 0.5 persistence (long-range positive correlations) α<0.5\alpha < 0.5 anti-persistence. plotdfa() draws log–log fluctuation plot fitted exponent:
code: fy <- dfa(dat$y, np = 40) head(fy) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>       s     F #>   <int> <dbl> #> 1    10  3.81 #> 2    11  5.01 #> 3    12  6.43 #> 4    13  8.09 #> 5    14 10.0  #> 6    15 12.2  # the DFA exponent is the slope of log F(s) against log s alpha <- coef(lm(log10(fy$F) ~ log10(fy$s)))[[2]] round(alpha, 3) #> [1] 3.014 plotdfa(fy, main = "DFA of y")

title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: Detrended Cross-Correlation Analysis (DCCA) (Podobnik Stanley 2008) replaces box variance detrended covariance FXY2(s)F_{XY}^2(s) two integrated profiles. FXY2(s)F_{XY}^2(s) unbounded, Zebende (2011) normalised DCCA cross-correlation coefficient ρDCCA(s)=FXY2(s)FX(s)FY(s)∈[−1,1], \rho_{\mathrm{DCCA}}(s) = \frac{F_{XY}^2(s)}{F_X(s)\,F_Y(s)} \[-1, 1],  scale--scale analogue Pearson’s correlation robust trends. rhodcca() returns one column per pair series:  coefficient strongly positive y–x2 pair, built simulation.

title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: several series interact, direct ρDCCA\rho_{\mathrm{DCCA}} may inflated shared drivers. Detrended Partial-Cross-Correlation Analysis (Yuan et al. 2015) removes linear influence remaining series inverting ρDCCA\rho_{\mathrm{DCCA}} matrix scale, giving partial coefficient ρDPCCA(s)\rho_{\mathrm{DPCCA}}(s):

title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: Detrended Multiple Cross-Correlation Coefficient (Zebende Silva Filho 2018) generalises multiple-correlation coefficient (RR) scales. Writing 𝛒(s)\boldsymbol{\rho}(s) matrix ρDCCA(s)\rho_{\mathrm{DCCA}}(s) among covariates 𝛒Y⋅(s)\boldsymbol{\rho}_{Y\cdot}(s) coefficients response, DMC(s)=𝛒Y⋅(s)⊤𝛒(s)−1𝛒Y⋅(s)∈[0,1], \mathrm{DMC}(s) = \boldsymbol{\rho}_{Y\cdot}(s)^{\top}\,                   \boldsymbol{\rho}(s)^{-1}\,                   \boldsymbol{\rho}_{Y\cdot}(s) \[0, 1],  measuring much response jointly explained covariates scale (recent statistical test given Wang, Xu, Fan (2021)):
code: dm <- dmc2(dat, np = 40) plot(dm[[1]], dm[[2]], type = "b", pch = 19, ylim = c(0, 1),      xlab = "scale s", ylab = "DMC(s)", main = "Detrended multiple correlation")

title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: Kristoufek (2015) turned detrended (co)variances full regression framework. Collecting detrended covariance matrix 𝐅(s)\mathbf{F}(s), scale-wise regression coefficients 𝛃(s)=𝐅XX(s)−1𝐅XY(s), \boldsymbol{\beta}(s) = \mathbf{F}_{XX}(s)^{-1}\,\mathbf{F}_{XY}(s),  unstandardised effect covariate response scale ss , unlike OLS, can vary across scales. multivariate case (p≥2p \ge 2 covariates, detrended multiple linear regression) due Shen (2015). betadfa() returns coefficients sbdfa() standardised counterparts (computed ρDCCA\rho_{\mathrm{DCCA}} matrices), Barreto et al. (2021) proposed scale-wise measure relative importance:  estimates hover around true coefficients 0.70.7 −0.5-0.5 every scale. fracreg() --one workhorse: single call returns scale vector, detrended (partial) correlations, raw standardised β(s)\beta(s), coefficient determination, DMC, confidence limits pp-values:
code: b  <- betadfa(dat, np = 40) head(b) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>       s    x2     x3 #>   <int> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1    10 0.577 -0.441 #> 2    11 0.568 -0.436 #> 3    12 0.561 -0.432 #> 4    13 0.555 -0.428 #> 5    14 0.550 -0.424 #> 6    15 0.545 -0.420  matplot(b$s, b[, c("x2", "x3")], type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2,         xlab = "scale s", ylab = expression(beta(s)),         main = "Scale-wise regression coefficients") abline(h = c(0.7, -0.5), lty = 3, col = "grey50") legend("right", c("x2", "x3"), col = 1:2, lty = 1, lwd = 2, bty = "n") fr <- fracreg(dat, dpo = 1, int = TRUE, np = 40) names(fr) #>  [1] "s"       "F"       "DCCA"    "DPCCA"   "BDFA"    "BSDFA"   "UDFA"    #>  [8] "VDFA"    "VDFA2"   "DMC2"    "R2DFA"   "UCIB"    "LCIB"    "p.value" #> [15] "TC"      "VIF"     "kappa"   "R2adj"

title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: effsizeDFA() reports scale-wise effect size introduced Barreto et al. (2021) — Cohen’s f2f^2(Cohen 1988) evaluated scale, f2=(Rfull2−Rreduced2)/(1−Rfull2)f^2 = (R^2_{\text{full}} - R^2_{\text{reduced}}) / (1 - R^2_{\text{full}}) — turning regression scale-resolved importance measure:

what: Inference: confidence intervals, diagnostics and robust standard errors
title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: point estimates half story. fracreg() also quantifies uncertainty every scale-wise coefficient: returned arrays carry variance (VDFA), 95% confidence limits (LCIB, UCIB) two-sided p.values, alongside collinearity diagnostics. coefficient indexed covariate jj scale kk — e.g. fr$BDFA[j, 1, k] fr$UCIB[j, 1, k].

previous_headings: Inference: confidence intervals, diagnostics and robust standard errors
title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: default (vcov = "inverse") variance built full inverse predictors’ detrended covariance matrix, var(βj(s))=Fε2(s)[𝐅XX(s)−1]jj, \operatorname{var}\!\big(\beta_j(s)\big)    = F^2_{\varepsilon}(s)\,\big[\mathbf{F}_{XX}(s)^{-1}\big]_{jj},  matrix produces 𝛃(s)\boldsymbol{\beta}(s)(Tilfani et al. 2022). legacy “marginal” variance Fε2(s)/FXj2(s)F^2_{\varepsilon}(s)/F^2_{X_j}(s) ignores -diagonal structure -covers covariates correlated; two agree orthogonal predictors. Pass vcov = "marginal" recover .
code: fr <- fracreg(dat, dpo = 1, int = TRUE, np = 40)   # vcov = "inverse" (default) b  <- fr$BDFA[1, 1, ]                               # x2 coefficient lo <- fr$LCIB[1, 1, ]; hi <- fr$UCIB[1, 1, ] plot(fr$s, b, type = "n", ylim = range(lo, hi),      xlab = "scale s", ylab = expression(beta[x2](s)),      main = "x2 coefficient with 95% confidence band") polygon(c(fr$s, rev(fr$s)), c(lo, rev(hi)), col = "grey85", border = NA) lines(fr$s, b, lwd = 2) abline(h = 0.7, lty = 3, col = "grey40")

previous_headings: Inference: confidence intervals, diagnostics and robust standard errors
title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: variance now depends conditioning 𝐅XX(s)\mathbf{F}_{XX}(s), fracreg() reports scale-wise diagnostics: variance inflation factors VIF, condition number kappa covariates’ scale-wise correlation matrix, adjusted coefficient determination R2adj (Tilfani et al. 2022). fracreg.diag() returns tidy table: two drivers independent, VIFs sit 1 kappa near 1; VIF rising well 1 scales flag scale-localised collinearity inflates coefficient variance — warning analogue ordinary regression.

previous_headings: Inference: confidence intervals, diagnostics and robust standard errors
what: Robust standard errors: HC and the wild bootstrap
title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: analytic variance assumes homoscedastic, independent detrended boxes. doubt, two robustifications available, built per-box detrended moments (DFA never recomputed): vcov = "HC" — heteroscedasticity-consistent (sandwich) variance, 𝐅XX−1𝛀𝐅XX−1\mathbf{F}_{XX}^{-1}\boldsymbol{\Omega}\,\mathbf{F}_{XX}^{-1} 𝛀\boldsymbol{\Omega} per-box outer product moment scores. coefficients unchanged — variance . Use overlap = FALSE. fracreg.WB() — wild bootstrap coefficients resamples scores returns percentile confidence limits pp-values. default weights = "dependent" dependent wild bootstrap Shao (2010) (robust dependence across boxes); "rademacher" "mammen" (Mammen 1993) give ..d. version, reproduces HC variance exactly.  analytic bootstrap bands keep x2 clearly zero every scale, confirming robust, scale-consistent positive effect.
code: fr_hc <- fracreg(dat, dpo = 1, int = TRUE, np = 40, overlap = FALSE, vcov = "HC") # HC standard error of the x2 coefficient (first few scales) round(sqrt(fr_hc$VDFA[1, 1, ])[1:6], 3) #> [1] 0.062 0.071 0.083 0.099 0.078 0.063 wb <- fracreg.WB(dat, B = 199, weights = "dependent", np = 20) plot(wb$s, wb$beta_x2, type = "n", ylim = range(wb$lower_x2, wb$upper_x2),      xlab = "scale s", ylab = expression(beta[x2](s)),      main = "x2 coefficient with wild-bootstrap 95% band") polygon(c(wb$s, rev(wb$s)), c(wb$lower_x2, rev(wb$upper_x2)),         col = "grey85", border = NA) lines(wb$s, wb$beta_x2, lwd = 2) abline(h = 0.7, lty = 3, col = "grey40")

title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: DFATools ships surrogate-based tests scale-wise coefficients, built phase-randomised surrogates series: fracreg.PStest() — Podobnik–Shen test H0:β(s)=0H_0\!: \beta(s) = 0 (detrended effect) (Podobnik et al. 2011; Shen 2015); fracreg.Ktest() — Kristoufek test H0:βDFA(s)=βOLSH_0\!: \beta_{\mathrm{DFA}}(s) = \beta_{\mathrm{OLS}} (scale effect equals global OLS one) (Kristoufek 2015); fracreg.IUTest() — intersection–union test Barreto et al. (2021) (following intersection–union principle, e.g. Van Deun et al. (2009)), combines two reject covariate direct, scale-dependent effect OLS miss. returns estimated β(s)\beta(s) together lower/upper critical bounds test, coefficient significant scale falls outside band. use small number surrogates speed; raise B practice.  x2 coefficient lies well null band scales — clear, scale-consistent effect.
code: ps <- fracreg.PStest(dat, B = 30, np = 30) head(ps) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>    bet1   bet2  slci1  slci2 suci1 suci2     s #>   <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <int> #> 1 0.577 -0.441 -0.538 -0.264 0.421 0.355    10 #> 2 0.568 -0.436 -0.574 -0.274 0.451 0.347    11 #> 3 0.561 -0.432 -0.615 -0.311 0.478 0.346    12 #> 4 0.555 -0.428 -0.660 -0.339 0.501 0.361    13 #> 5 0.550 -0.424 -0.706 -0.360 0.514 0.374    14 #> 6 0.545 -0.420 -0.750 -0.393 0.523 0.386    15 plot(ps$s, ps$bet1, type = "l", lwd = 2,      ylim = range(ps[, c("bet1", "slci1", "suci1")]),      xlab = "scale s", ylab = expression(beta[x2](s)),      main = "x2 coefficient vs Podobnik-Shen null band") lines(ps$s, ps$slci1, lty = 2, col = "red") lines(ps$s, ps$suci1, lty = 2, col = "red")

title: Detrended fluctuation analysis and related methods
text: Choose np implied range box sizes care: small boxes noisy large boxes leave windows. defaults span box sizes 10 one fifth series length. Use int = TRUE noise-like (stationary) inputs int = FALSE series already integrated (random-walk-like). confidence intervals, keep default vcov = "inverse"; switch wild bootstrap (fracreg.WB()) suspect heteroscedasticity dependence boxes, read VIF/kappa diagnostics spot scales collinearity inflates variance. fracreg() fracreg.WB() accept abs = TRUE build cross-correlation step absolute detrended covariance, robust outliers. surrogate tests computationally heavy (refit model B times); start small increase B workflow settled.

what: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis and Related Methods
title: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis and Related Methods
text: DFATools toolbox Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (DFA) methods measuring long-range correlations, cross-correlations regression nonstationary time series, classical autocorrelation ordinary regression biased trends. provides: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis — dfa() Detrended Cross-Correlation Analysis rho-DCCA coefficient — rhodcca() Detrended Partial Cross-Correlation coefficient — rhodpcca() Detrended Multiple Cross-Correlation coefficient (DMC) — dmc2() Detrended fractal regression: scale-wise standardized coefficients — betadfa(), sbdfa(), fracreg() Scale-wise f2f^2effect sizes — effsizeDFA() Significance tests: Podobnik-Shen, Kristoufek intersection-union — fracreg.PStest(), fracreg.Ktest(), fracreg.IUTest() scale-dependent standardized coefficients, scale-wise effect size intersection-union test follow Barreto et al. (2021) doi:10.1016/j.physa.2021.126259.

title: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis and Related Methods

title: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis and Related Methods
text: response y driven covariate x2, random-walk-like: See vignette("DFATools") full theory worked examples.
code: library(DFATools) #> Registered S3 method overwritten by 'quantmod': #>   method            from #>   as.zoo.data.frame zoo  set.seed(1) x2  <- cumsum(rnorm(800)) y   <- 0.7 * x2 + cumsum(rnorm(800)) dat <- data.frame(y = y, x2 = x2)  # DFA fluctuation function of y head(dfa(y, np = 30)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>       s     F #>   <int> <dbl> #> 1    10  3.50 #> 2    11  4.65 #> 3    12  6.03 #> 4    13  7.68 #> 5    14  9.62 #> 6    15 11.9  # scale-wise rho-DCCA cross-correlation coefficient head(rhodcca(dat, np = 30)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>       s DCCA12 #>   <int>  <dbl> #> 1    10  0.487 #> 2    11  0.471 #> 3    12  0.457 #> 4    13  0.445 #> 5    14  0.434 #> 6    15  0.426

Multiple Fractal Regression — fracreg
text: Calculates scale-dependent (DFA-based) multiple linear regression: coefficients, variance confidence intervals scale, together scale-wise collinearity diagnostics.

title: Multiple Fractal Regression — fracreg
code: fracreg(   data,   dpo,   int,   np = 91,   overlap = TRUE,   vcov = c("inverse", "marginal", "HC"),   abs = FALSE )

title: Multiple Fractal Regression — fracreg
text: data matrix data frame time series; first column response remaining columns predictors. dpo detrending polynomial order. int logical. TRUE integration process applied. np number point scales. overlap logical. TRUE overlapping windows applied. vcov coefficient-variance estimator: "inverse" (default; full \(F_{XX}(s)^{-1}\), Tilfani et al. 2022), "marginal" (legacy \(1/F^2_{X_j}(s)\), Shen 2015), "HC" (experimental heteroscedasticity-consistent sandwich built per-box detrended moment scores; overlap = FALSE recommended finite-sample normalisation still calibrated). abs logical. TRUE absolute detrended covariance used cross-correlation step (robust outliers).

title: Multiple Fractal Regression — fracreg
text: list , among others: scale s, detrended fluctuation   F, DCCA, DPCCA, beta estimates BDFA,   standardized betas BSDFA, residual variance UDFA, coefficient   variance VDFA, multiple correlation DMC2, R2DFA,   confidence limits UCIB/LCIB, p.value, critical value   TC, scale-wise diagnostics VIF, condition number   kappa adjusted R2adj.

title: Multiple Fractal Regression — fracreg
text: variance scale-dependent coefficients follows Tilfani et al. (2022, Eq. 25), \(\mathrm{var}(\hat\beta_j(s)) = F^2_\varepsilon(s) [F_{XX}(s)^{-1}]_{jj}\), .e. uses full inverse detrended covariance matrix predictors, consistent coefficients estimated. collinearity differs legacy "marginal" form \(F^2_\varepsilon(s) / F^2_{X_j}(s)\) (-covers); two coincide orthogonal predictors. Set vcov = "marginal" reproduce legacy behaviour.

title: Multiple Fractal Regression — fracreg
text: Barreto, . D. C., Dore, L. H., Stosic, T. Stosic, B. D. (2021). Extending DFA-based multiple linear regression inference: application acoustic impedance models. Physica , 582, 126259. Tilfani, O., Kristoufek, L., Ferreira, P. El Boukfaoui, M. Y. (2022). Heterogeneity economic relationships: scale dependence multivariate fractal regression. Physica , 588, 126530. Shen, C. (2015). new detrended semipartial cross-correlation analysis. Physics Letters , 379(44), 2962-2969. Kristoufek, L. (2015). Detrended fluctuation analysis regression framework: estimating dependence different scales. Physical Review E, 91(2), 022802.

title: Podobnik-Shen Test — fracreg.PStest
text: Podobnik, B., Jiang, Z.-Q., Zhou, W.-X. Stanley, H. E. (2011). Statistical tests power-law cross-correlated processes. Physical Review E, 84(6), 066118. Shen, C. (2015). new detrended semipartial cross-correlation analysis. Physics Letters , 379(44), 2962-2969.

what: Wild bootstrap inference for the fractal regression — fracreg.WB
title: Wild bootstrap inference for the fractal regression — fracreg.WB
text: Wild-bootstrap confidence intervals significance scale-dependent regression coefficients. resamples per-box detrended-moment scores (scores used fracreg(vcov = "HC")), robust heteroscedasticity – dependent weights – dependence boxes, recompute DFA replicate.

title: Wild bootstrap inference for the fractal regression — fracreg.WB
code: fracreg.WB(   data,   B = 999,   weights = c("dependent", "rademacher", "mammen"),   bandwidth = NULL,   dpo = 1,   int = TRUE,   np = 91,   overlap = FALSE,   abs = FALSE )

title: Wild bootstrap inference for the fractal regression — fracreg.WB
text: data matrix data frame; first column response, rest predictors. B number bootstrap replicates. weights multiplier-weight scheme: "dependent" (Shao 2010), "rademacher" "mammen". bandwidth kernel bandwidth "dependent" weights; defaults \(T_s^{1/3}\) per scale. dpo detrending polynomial order. int logical. TRUE integration process applied. np number point scales. overlap logical. TRUE overlapping windows used (non-overlapping boxes, default, recommended resampling). abs logical. TRUE absolute detrended covariance used.

title: Wild bootstrap inference for the fractal regression — fracreg.WB
text: tibble scale s , per predictor, estimate   (beta_*), lower/upper interval bounds (lower_*,   upper_*) p-value (p_*), one row per scale.

title: Wild bootstrap inference for the fractal regression — fracreg.WB
text: scale \(s\) \(T_s\) boxes, scores \(r_v\) inverse \(F_{XX}(s)^{-1}\), replicate \(\hat\beta^*_b(s) = \hat\beta(s) + F_{XX}(s)^{-1}\,(1/T_s)\sum_v r_v w_{v,b}\). interval 2.5% / 97.5% quantiles \(\hat\beta^*_b(s)\); two-sided p-value tests \(H_0\!: \beta_j(s) = 0\). "dependent" weights (Shao 2010) keep coverage boxes dependent (e.g. strong long memory), analytic t ..d. weights can fail.

title: Wild bootstrap inference for the fractal regression — fracreg.WB
text: Shao, X. (2010). dependent wild bootstrap. Journal American Statistical Association, 105(489), 218-235. Mammen, E. (1993). Bootstrap wild bootstrap high dimensional linear models. Annals Statistics, 21(1), 255-285. Tilfani, O., Kristoufek, L., Ferreira, P. El Boukfaoui, M. Y. (2022). Heterogeneity economic relationships: scale dependence multivariate fractal regression. Physica , 588, 126530.

title: Wild bootstrap inference for the fractal regression — fracreg.WB

title: DFATools 1.0.0
text: First public release. DFATools continuation, new name, development package previously called DFAmethods2; collects Detrended Fluctuation Analysis related scale-dependent methods one place.

title: DFATools 1.0.0
text: Detrended fluctuation analysis (dfa) detrended cross-correlation analysis, detrended cross-correlation partial cross-correlation coefficients (rhodcca, rhodpcca) detrended multiple correlation (dmc2). Scale-dependent (fractal) multiple regression fracreg(), scale-dependent standardized coefficients, scale-wise effect size (effsizeDFA) smooth coefficient profile (betadfa, sbdfa). doi:10.1016/j.physa.2021.126259.

title: DFATools 1.0.0
text: coefficient variance estimated full inverse detrended covariance matrix predictors, var(beta_j(s)) = F2_eps(s) * [F_XX(s)^-1]_jj (Tilfani et al. 2022, Eq. 25), consistent coefficients estimated. legacy “marginal” form, F2_eps(s) / F2_Xj(s), -covers multicollinearity; two coincide orthogonal predictors. vcov = c("inverse", "marginal",   "HC") selects estimator (default "inverse"). vcov = "HC" experimental heteroscedasticity-consistent (sandwich) estimator built per-box detrended-moment scores; overlap = FALSE recommended. Scale-wise collinearity diagnostics returned: variance inflation factors $VIF, condition number $kappa predictors’ scale-wise correlation matrix, adjusted coefficient determination $R2adj (Tilfani et al. 2022, Eq. 26). fracreg.diag() returns tidy tibble. fracreg.WB() gives wild-bootstrap confidence intervals p-values scale-dependent coefficients, robust heteroscedasticity (default "dependent" weights, Shao 2010) dependence boxes. resamples per-box moment scores, recompute DFA per replicate; ..d. weights reproduce vcov = "HC" sandwich variance. abs uses absolute detrended covariance cross-correlation step (robust outliers).
